items.title: Cuba sent doctors. Washington sent a destroyer.
items.summary: The US blockade is killing not just a failing state, but six decades of a small island extending global solidarity.

items.title: Refugee in my own city: Surviving Tehran’s bombing, with my cat for company
items.summary: Despite the relentless bombing, Sana refuses to flee Tehran, clinging to resilience and fleeting moments of normalcy.

items.title: Ghanaian footballer Dominic Frimpong killed in armed attack on team bus
items.summary: Berekum Chelsea's Dominic Frimpong died when armed robbers attacked the Ghana Premier League team travelling home.

items.title: Australia appoints female army chief for the first time in history
items.summary: Lieutenant General Susan Coyle has held several senior command roles over a nearly 40-year military career.

items.title: Why oil prices aren’t what you think – and what it means for global supply
items.summary: Growing gap between physical and on-paper oil prices points to more serious energy shock than appreciated, analysts say.

items.title: Celebrations in Hungary after Orban ousted in landmark election
items.summary: Celebrations broke out across Budapest after opposition leader Peter Magyar defeated Prime Minister Viktor Orban.

items.title: Pope Leo heads to Algeria as he starts four-country Africa tour
items.summary: The pope will also visit Cameroon, Angola and Equatorial Guinea during his 11-day trip to the continent.

items.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 45 of the US-Iran conflict?
items.summary: The move to blockade the Strait of Hormuz is the latest escalation in the war after failure to sign peace deal.

items.title: Who is Yechiel Leiter, Israel’s US envoy leading talks with Lebanon?
items.summary: Israel's envoy to the US has far-right ties and has engaged in contentious rhetoric during Lebanese peace negotiations.

items.title: Sinner beats Alcaraz in straight sets to win Monte Carlo, reclaim No 1 spot
items.summary: Jannik Sinner continues his title-winning run with a 7-6(5), 6-3 triumph and grabs top spot in ATP rankings.

items.title: ‘Terrible for foreign policy’: Trump attacks Pope Leo after peace appeal
items.summary: Leo, ​who last year became the first US-born pope, has emerged as an outspoken critic of the US-Israeli ⁠war on Iran.

items.title: Viktor Orban swept from power after 16 years ruling Hungary

items.summary: The Northern Irishman becomes the first player to repeat at Augusta National since Tiger Woods back in 2001-2002.

items.summary: Asian stocks fall as naval blockade threat injects new turmoil into financial markets.

items.title: US military threatens to blockade all Iranian ports starting on Monday
items.summary: Vessels will still be able to transit the Strait of Hormuz to and from non-Iranian ports, says US Central Command.

items.summary: CENTCOM says the blockade will begin at 14:00 GMT, as Iran warns of even higher prices at the pump.

items.title: Peter Magyar says his election win has ‘liberated Hungary’ from Orban

items.title: These are people Israel killed in Lebanon on a single day
items.summary: In a matter of minutes, Israel killed more than 350 people across Lebanon on Wednesday, April 8.

items.title: Eric Swalwell exits California governor race after sex assault allegations

items.title: World reacts to Peter Magyar defeating Viktor Orban, Hungary’s longtime PM
items.summary: Prime Minister Viktor Orban concedes defeat in election as results show Peter Magyar's Tisza party winning by landslide.

items.title: History of flotilla campaigns to end Israel’s siege of Gaza
items.summary: A 70-boat flotilla sets sail from Spain, aiming to break Israel's naval blockade on Gaza amid history of interceptions.

items.title: ‘I’ve congratulated the victorious party’: Viktor Orban concedes
